hero.headline: Technical backend developer, test-driven and focused on continuous system evolution.
hero.summary: I build backend solutions with Java (LTS 21 and 25), strong SQL, and automated tests. I have experience integrating Java + Python for logistics optimization, with a focus on object-oriented architecture and code quality.
hero.role: Backend developer in training, focused on TDD and optimization.

thesis.pre: In 2024, I was learning to code. In 2026,

about.lead: This portfolio gathers what I've learned shipping backend in production: what I stand for, how I decide, and where I've applied it.
paragraphs: I'm a software engineering intern at Grupo.Con / Doctor.Con (accounting), owning fiscal automations (RPA on the gov.br portal), integrations with tax APIs, data enrichment with generative models, and the company's CI/CD infrastructure.

Before that, I spent 18 months in customer support for Mercado Livre — 30 cases per day on average, NPS consistently above 30. Before that, I played Rainbow Six Siege professionally for Yeah Gaming and Isurus Gaming, national runner-up in the 2019/20 season.
I'm a 7th-semester Computer Science student at IFNMG and open to opportunities in test-driven backend, automation or integrations.

items.label: NPS sustained for 18 months at AeC

items.title: Água Viva OOP
items.stack: OOP backend · relational store · external solver · container orchestration
bullets: Decoupled architecture with dedicated solver consumed via REST.
Full integration test suite against a real DB in tmpfs — no mocks.
Domain with immutable Value Objects and roles modeled as enums.

items.title: Metaheuristics for Christofides
items.kind: Comparative study of metaheuristics on a Christofides variant.
bullets: ACO, Physarum, Tabu Search and Genetic implemented from scratch.
Hybrid strategy combining techniques on the hardest instances.
Comparative analysis: solution quality vs. wall-clock time.

items.title: Algorithms & Data Structures
items.kind: From-scratch implementation of classical structures and algorithms.
bullets: Hash tables, trees, linked lists, classical search and sort.
Study of heuristics applied to optimization problems.

items.title: OSA — File Systems
items.kind: B-trees and serialization for optimized disk→memory loading.

items.title: TBO — Search & Sort
items.kind: KMP, mergesort, binary search, hash and direct mapping.

items.title: Portfolio
items.kind: Editorial site about how I work with backend.

stack.section_meta: How I work — not which frameworks I use

groups.title: Test-driven development
groups.manifesto: I'd rather write the test first, even if it looks slow. Slow tests find fast bugs. I distrust mocks at critical boundaries and prefer real databases, real integrations, whenever I can.
contexts: TDD in backend, automation and parsing
Integration against real databases in controlled environments
Coverage as a consequence, not a target

groups.title: Backend systems
groups.manifesto: Well-named code beats a comment. I prefer rich domain with immutable value objects to anemic objects fed by a magic ORM. Relational modeling is part of the design, not an infra detail.
contexts: Modeling with partial indexes, CTEs and views
Role and state hierarchies modeled as enums

groups.title: Automation & integrations
groups.manifesto: A manual task that shows up three times becomes script-worthy. Integration with a public API needs a fallback and a readable log, not just the happy path.
contexts: RPA on government and tax portals
Integration with fiscal APIs and data enrichment
Web scraping with a refinement step before persistence

groups.title: Continuous delivery
groups.manifesto: Deploy is part of the product. A pipeline nobody understands is a broken pipeline. Logs should speak the language of whoever wakes up at 3am to read them.
contexts: CI pipelines with deploy on push
Continuous deploy over SSH to VPS

groups.title: Fundamentals
groups.manifesto: The right data structure separates a solution from a hack that scales. The right algorithm on the first try saves weeks of rewriting.
contexts: Classical structures and algorithms implemented from scratch
Heuristics and metaheuristics on NP problems

groups.title: Pressure & communication
groups.manifesto: A national runner-up in esports and 18 months handling high-demand customer support taught me to keep my head cool, decide with little information, and translate technical constraints into language for whoever has to call the shot.
contexts: Documentation someone else can use without me

quote.text: I'd rather write the test first, even if it looks slow. Slow tests find fast bugs.

bullets: RPA for automated tax document issuance on a government portal, eliminating recurring manual work in the tax department.
Invoice issuance control flow integrated with a fiscal API.
Co-authored architecture for enriching registration data with generative models and web scraping, with a refinement step before persistence.
Owner of CI/CD infrastructure: pipelines with continuous deploy to VPS.
Inbound ticket support averaging 30 cases per day.
Technical diagnosis before escalation, identifying recurring failure patterns.
Translating technical constraints into practical solutions across customer profiles.

entries.location: Yeah Gaming / Isurus Gaming · São Paulo, Brazil
bullets: League standings leader for most of the season.
Team of 5 under competitive pressure, real-time technical communication.
Step-by-step decision making in a high-variance environment.
